revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 佐波神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|46|38.3|N|138|46|14|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[西伊豆町]]仁科1870 |位置 = {{Coord|34|46|38.3|N|138|46|14|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[八幡神|廣幡八幡大神]]、[[積羽八重事代主命]] |社格 = [[伊豆国の式内社一覧#式内社|式内社]]、旧[[郷社]] |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 11月2日〜3日 |神事 = }} '''佐波神社'''（さわじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[西伊豆町]]仁科にある[[神社]]。[[伊豆国の式内社一覧#式内社|伊豆国の式内社]]の1つである<ref name="yuisho">境内由緒書</ref>。
== 概要 == 創建不詳だが、[[式内社]]なので『[[延喜式神名帳]]』が作られた[[延長]]5年（[[927年]]）以前に遡る<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/神名帳-82644 神名帳とは] - [[コトバンク]]</ref>。
社名の「佐波（さわ）」は地名の「澤/沢」と同語源<ref name="yuisho"/>。
元々は三島明神と八幡宮が海辺（現・仁科港/鴨ケ池遺跡）に並立していたが、津波の影響で[[慶長]]14年（[[1609年]]）に現在地に移され、[[文政]]6年（[[1823年]]）3月に一社に合祀された<ref name="yuisho"/>。
11月2日〜3日の例大祭では、伊豆地区で5ヶ所（町内では3ヶ所）しか残っていない、県指定無形民俗文化財である「人形[[三番叟]]」が披露される<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/sawasanbasou 佐波神社三番叟] - 西伊豆町観光協会</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021026.html しずおか文化財ナビ 仁科の人形三番叟] - 静岡県</ref>。
== 祭神 == * [[八幡神|廣幡八幡大神]] * [[積羽八重事代主命]]
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:さわしんしや}} [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:西伊豆町]]

{{神社 |名称 = 佐波神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|46|38.3|N|138|46|14|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[西伊豆町]]仁科1870 |位置 = {{Coord|34|46|38.3|N|138|46|14|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[八幡神|廣幡八幡大神]]、[[積羽八重事代主命]] |社格 = [[伊豆国の式内社一覧#式内社|式内社]]、旧[[郷社]] |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 11月2日〜3日 |神事 = }} '''佐波神社'''（さわじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[西伊豆町]]仁科にある[[神社]]。[[伊豆国の式内社一覧#式内社|伊豆国の式内社]]の1つである<ref name="yuisho">境内由緒書</ref>。
== 概要 == 創建不詳だが、[[式内社]]なので『[[延喜式神名帳]]』が作られた[[延長]]5年（[[927年]]）以前に遡る<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/神名帳-82644 神名帳とは] - [[コトバンク]]</ref>。
社名の「佐波（さわ）」は地名の「澤/沢」と同語源<ref name="yuisho"/>。
元々は三島明神と八幡宮が海辺（現・仁科港/鴨ケ池遺跡）に並立していたが、津波の影響で[[慶長]]14年（[[1609年]]）に現在地に移され、[[文政]]6年（[[1823年]]）3月に一社に合祀された<ref name="yuisho"/>。
11月2日〜3日の例大祭では、伊豆地区で5ヶ所（町内では3ヶ所）のみが伝承する、県指定無形民俗文化財である「人形[[三番叟]]」が披露される<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/sawasanbasou 佐波神社三番叟] - 西伊豆町観光協会</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021026.html 仁科の人形三番叟] - 静岡県</ref>。（ちなみに、残る4ヶ所は、町内では宇久須地区の牛越(うしこし)神社と、仁科(中)地区の(海名野)神明神社の2ヶ所、町外では南伊豆町子浦の伊鈴川(五十鈴川/いすずがわ)神社と、伊豆市中伊豆地区の小川神社の2ヶ所<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/ushisanbasou 牛越神社人形三番叟] - 西伊豆町観光協会</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021027.html 海名野神明神社の人形三番叟] - 静岡県</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021025.html 人形三番叟] - 静岡県</ref>。）
== 祭神 == * [[八幡神|廣幡八幡大神]] * [[積羽八重事代主命]]
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:さわしんしや}} [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:西伊豆町]]

社名の「佐波（さわ）」は地名の「澤/沢」と同語源<ref name="yuisho"/>。
元々は三島明神と八幡宮が海辺（現・仁科港/鴨ケ池遺跡）に並立していたが、津波の影響で[[慶長]]14年（[[1609年]]）に現在地に移され、[[文政]]6年（[[1823年]]）3月に一社に合祀された<ref name="yuisho"/>。
11月2日〜3日の例大祭では、伊豆地区で5ヶ所（町内では3ヶ所）のみが伝承する、県指定無形民俗文化財である「人形[[三番叟]]」が披露される<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/sawasanbasou 佐波神社三番叟] - 西伊豆町観光協会</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021026.html 仁科の人形三番叟] - 静岡県</ref>。（ちなみに、残る4ヶ所は、町内では宇久須地区の牛越(うしこし)神社と、仁科(中)地区の(海名野)神明神社の2ヶ所、町外では南伊豆町子浦の伊鈴川(五十鈴川/いすずがわ)神社と、伊豆市中伊豆地区の小川神社の2ヶ所<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/ushisanbasou 牛越神社人形三番叟] - 西伊豆町観光協会</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021027.html 海名野神明神社の人形三番叟] - 静岡県</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021025.html 人形三番叟] - 静岡県</ref>。）
== 祭神 == * [[八幡神|廣幡八幡大神]] * [[積羽八重事代主命]]
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:さわしんしや}} [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:西伊豆町の建築物]] [[Category:西伊豆町の歴史]]

{{nihongo|'''Sawa Shrine'''|佐波神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sawa Shrine'''|佐波神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{Expand Japanese|佐波神社 (西伊豆町)}}

{{Infobox religious building

{{nihongo|'''Sawa Shrine'''|佐波神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sawa Shrine'''|佐波神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

revision.comment: 6 revisions imported
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 佐波神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|46|38.3|N|138|46|14|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[西伊豆町]]仁科1870 |位置 = {{Coord|34|46|38.3|N|138|46|14|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[八幡神|廣幡八幡大神]]、[[積羽八重事代主命]] |社格 = [[伊豆国の式内社一覧#式内社|式内社]]、旧[[郷社]] |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 11月2日〜3日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=佐波神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|46|38.3|N|138|46|14|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''佐波神社'''（さわじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[西伊豆町]]仁科にある[[神社]]。[[伊豆国の式内社一覧#式内社|伊豆国の式内社]]の1つである<ref name="yuisho">境内由緒書</ref>。
== 概要 == 創建不詳だが、[[式内社]]なので『[[延喜式神名帳]]』が作られた[[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）以前に遡る<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/神名帳-82644 神名帳とは] - [[コトバンク]]</ref>。
社名の「佐波（さわ）」は地名の「澤/沢」と同語源<ref name="yuisho"/>。
元々は三島明神と八幡宮が海辺（現・仁科港/鴨ケ池遺跡）に並立していたが、津波の影響で[[慶長]]14年（[[1609年]]）に現在地に移され、[[文政]]6年（[[1823年]]）3月に一社に合祀された<ref name="yuisho"/>。
11月2日〜3日の例大祭では、伊豆地区で5ヶ所（町内では3ヶ所）のみが伝承する、県指定無形民俗文化財である「人形[[三番叟]]」が披露される<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/sawasanbasou 佐波神社三番叟] - 西伊豆町観光協会</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021026.html 仁科の人形三番叟] - 静岡県</ref>。（ちなみに、残る4ヶ所は、町内では宇久須地区の牛越(うしこし)神社と、仁科(中)地区の(海名野)神明神社の2ヶ所、町外では南伊豆町子浦の伊鈴川(五十鈴川/いすずがわ)神社と、伊豆市中伊豆地区の小川神社の2ヶ所<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/ushisanbasou 牛越神社人形三番叟] - 西伊豆町観光協会</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021027.html 海名野神明神社の人形三番叟] - 静岡県</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021025.html 人形三番叟] - 静岡県</ref>。）
== 祭神 == * [[八幡神|廣幡八幡大神]] * [[積羽八重事代主命]]
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:さわしんしや}} [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:西伊豆町の建築物]] [[Category:西伊豆町の歴史]]

== 概要 == 創建不詳だが、[[式内社]]なので『[[延喜式神名帳]]』が作られた[[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）以前に遡る<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/神名帳-82644 神名帳とは] - [[コトバンク]]</ref>。
社名の「佐波（さわ）」は地名の「澤/沢」と同語源<ref name="yuisho"/>。
元々は三島明神と八幡宮が海辺（現・仁科港/鴨ケ池遺跡）に並立していたが、津波の影響で[[慶長]]14年（[[1609年]]）に現在地に移され、[[文政]]6年（[[1823年]]）3月に一社に合祀された<ref name="yuisho"/>。
11月2日〜3日の例大祭では、伊豆地区で5ヶ所（町内では3ヶ所）のみが伝承する、県指定無形民俗文化財である「人形[[三番叟]]」が披露される<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/sawasanbasou 佐波神社三番叟] - 西伊豆町観光協会</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021026.html 仁科の人形三番叟] - 静岡県</ref>。（ちなみに、残る4ヶ所は、町内では宇久須地区の牛越(うしこし)神社と、仁科(中)地区の(海名野)神明神社の2ヶ所、町外では南伊豆町子浦の伊鈴川(五十鈴川/いすずがわ)神社と、伊豆市中伊豆地区の小川神社の2ヶ所<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/ushisanbasou 牛越神社人形三番叟] - 西伊豆町観光協会</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021027.html 海名野神明神社の人形三番叟] - 静岡県</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021025.html 人形三番叟] - 静岡県</ref>。）

[[JA:佐波神社 (西伊豆町)]]
{{wikidata link|Q123179471}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]
{{translated page|ja|佐波神社 (西伊豆町)|version=103426833|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]]
== Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 佐波神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|46|38.3|N|138|46|14|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[西伊豆町]]仁科1870 |位置 = {{Coord|34|46|38.3|N|138|46|14|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[八幡神|廣幡八幡大神]]、[[積羽八重事代主命]] |社格 = [[伊豆国の式内社一覧#式内社|式内社]]、旧[[郷社]] |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 11月2日〜3日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=佐波神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|46|38.3|N|138|46|14|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''佐波神社'''（さわじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[西伊豆町]]仁科にある[[神社]]。[[伊豆国の式内社一覧#式内社|伊豆国の式内社]]の1つである<ref name="yuisho">境内由緒書</ref>。
== 概要 == 創建不詳だが、[[式内社]]なので『[[延喜式神名帳]]』が作られた[[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）以前に遡る<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/神名帳-82644 神名帳とは] - [[コトバンク]]</ref>。
社名の「佐波（さわ）」は地名の「澤/沢」と同語源<ref name="yuisho"/>。
元々は三島明神と八幡宮が海辺（現・仁科港/鴨ケ池遺跡）に並立していたが、津波の影響で[[慶長]]14年（[[1609年]]）に現在地に移され、[[文政]]6年（[[1823年]]）3月に一社に合祀された<ref name="yuisho"/>。
11月2日〜3日の例大祭では、伊豆地区で5ヶ所（町内では3ヶ所）のみが伝承する、県指定無形民俗文化財である「人形[[三番叟]]」が披露される<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/sawasanbasou 佐波神社三番叟] - 西伊豆町観光協会</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021026.html 仁科の人形三番叟] - 静岡県</ref>。（ちなみに、残る4ヶ所は、町内では宇久須地区の牛越(うしこし)神社と、仁科(中)地区の(海名野)神明神社の2ヶ所、町外では南伊豆町子浦の伊鈴川(五十鈴川/いすずがわ)神社と、伊豆市中伊豆地区の小川神社の2ヶ所<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/ushisanbasou 牛越神社人形三番叟] - 西伊豆町観光協会</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021027.html 海名野神明神社の人形三番叟] - 静岡県</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021025.html 人形三番叟] - 静岡県</ref>。）
== 祭神 == * [[八幡神|廣幡八幡大神]] * [[積羽八重事代主命]]
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
[[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:西伊豆町の建築物]] [[Category:西伊豆町の歴史]]
==End Japanese==

{{translated page|ja|佐波神社 (西伊豆町)|version=103426833|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]]
== Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 佐波神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|46|38.3|N|138|46|14|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[西伊豆町]]仁科1870 |位置 = {{Coord|34|46|38.3|N|138|46|14|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[八幡神|廣幡八幡大神]]、[[積羽八重事代主命]] |社格 = [[伊豆国の式内社一覧#式内社|式内社]]、旧[[郷社]] |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 11月2日〜3日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=佐波神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|46|38.3|N|138|46|14|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''佐波神社'''（さわじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[西伊豆町]]仁科にある[[神社]]。[[伊豆国の式内社一覧#式内社|伊豆国の式内社]]の1つである<ref name="yuisho">境内由緒書</ref>。
== 概要 == 創建不詳だが、[[式内社]]なので『[[延喜式神名帳]]』が作られた[[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）以前に遡る<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/神名帳-82644 神名帳とは] - [[コトバンク]]</ref>。
社名の「佐波（さわ）」は地名の「澤/沢」と同語源<ref name="yuisho"/>。
元々は三島明神と八幡宮が海辺（現・仁科港/鴨ケ池遺跡）に並立していたが、津波の影響で[[慶長]]14年（[[1609年]]）に現在地に移され、[[文政]]6年（[[1823年]]）3月に一社に合祀された<ref name="yuisho"/>。
11月2日〜3日の例大祭では、伊豆地区で5ヶ所（町内では3ヶ所）のみが伝承する、県指定無形民俗文化財である「人形[[三番叟]]」が披露される<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/sawasanbasou 佐波神社三番叟] - 西伊豆町観光協会</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021026.html 仁科の人形三番叟] - 静岡県</ref>。（ちなみに、残る4ヶ所は、町内では宇久須地区の牛越(うしこし)神社と、仁科(中)地区の(海名野)神明神社の2ヶ所、町外では南伊豆町子浦の伊鈴川(五十鈴川/いすずがわ)神社と、伊豆市中伊豆地区の小川神社の2ヶ所<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/ushisanbasou 牛越神社人形三番叟] - 西伊豆町観光協会</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021027.html 海名野神明神社の人形三番叟] - 静岡県</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021025.html 人形三番叟] - 静岡県</ref>。）
== 祭神 == * [[八幡神|廣幡八幡大神]] * [[積羽八重事代主命]]
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
==End Japanese== [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:西伊豆町の建築物]] [[Category:西伊豆町の歴史]]

'''佐波神社'''（さわじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|西伊豆町|Q1203949}}仁科にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|伊豆国の式内社一覧#式内社|Q11380552|伊豆国の式内社}}の1つである<ref name="yuisho">境内由緒書</ref>。

== 概要 ==
創建不詳だが、{{jalink|式内社}}なので『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』が作られた{{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）以前に遡る<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/神名帳-82644 神名帳とは] - {{jalink|コトバンク|Q11302260}}</ref>。

社名の「佐波（さわ）」は地名の「澤/沢」と同語源<ref name="yuisho"/>。

元々は三島明神と八幡宮が海辺（現・仁科港/鴨ケ池遺跡）に並立していたが、津波の影響で{{jalink|慶長|Q1328185}}14年（{{jalink|1609年|Q6762}}）に現在地に移され、{{jalink|文政|Q1327119}}6年（{{jalink|1823年|Q6988}}）3月に一社に合祀された<ref name="yuisho"/>。

11月2日〜3日の例大祭では、伊豆地区で5ヶ所（町内では3ヶ所）のみが伝承する、県指定無形民俗文化財である「人形{{jalink|三番叟|Q11356796}}」が披露される<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/sawasanbasou 佐波神社三番叟] - 西伊豆町観光協会</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021026.html 仁科の人形三番叟] - 静岡県</ref>。（ちなみに、残る4ヶ所は、町内では宇久須地区の牛越(うしこし)神社と、仁科(中)地区の(海名野)神明神社の2ヶ所、町外では南伊豆町子浦の伊鈴川(五十鈴川/いすずがわ)神社と、伊豆市中伊豆地区の小川神社の2ヶ所<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/ushisanbasou 牛越神社人形三番叟] - 西伊豆町観光協会</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021027.html 海名野神明神社の人形三番叟] - 静岡県</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021025.html 人形三番叟] - 静岡県</ref>。）

{{wikidata link|Q123179471}}

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = 佐波神社
|画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|46|38.3|N|138|46|14|E}}}}
|所在地 = {{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|西伊豆町|Q1203949}}仁科1870
|位置 = {{Coord|34|46|38.3|N|138|46|14|E|display=inline,title}}
|祭神   = {{jalink|八幡神|Q261637|廣幡八幡大神}}、{{jalink|積羽八重事代主命}}
|社格   = {{jalink|伊豆国の式内社一覧#式内社|Q11380552|式内社}}、旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}
|創建   = 
|本殿   = 
|別名   = 
|札所等 = 
|例祭   = 11月2日〜3日
|神事   = 
|地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=佐波神社|coordinates=
{{coord2|34|46|38.3|N|138|46|14|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
}}
'''佐波神社'''（さわじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|西伊豆町|Q1203949}}仁科にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|伊豆国の式内社一覧#式内社|Q11380552|伊豆国の式内社}}の1つである<ref name="yuisho">境内由緒書</ref>。

=== 概要 ===
創建不詳だが、{{jalink|式内社}}なので『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』が作られた{{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）以前に遡る<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/神名帳-82644 神名帳とは] - {{jalink|コトバンク|Q11302260}}</ref>。

社名の「佐波（さわ）」は地名の「澤/沢」と同語源<ref name="yuisho"/>。

元々は三島明神と八幡宮が海辺（現・仁科港/鴨ケ池遺跡）に並立していたが、津波の影響で{{jalink|慶長|Q1328185}}14年（{{jalink|1609年|Q6762}}）に現在地に移され、{{jalink|文政|Q1327119}}6年（{{jalink|1823年|Q6988}}）3月に一社に合祀された<ref name="yuisho"/>。

11月2日〜3日の例大祭では、伊豆地区で5ヶ所（町内では3ヶ所）のみが伝承する、県指定無形民俗文化財である「人形{{jalink|三番叟|Q11356796}}」が披露される<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/sawasanbasou 佐波神社三番叟] - 西伊豆町観光協会</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021026.html 仁科の人形三番叟] - 静岡県</ref>。（ちなみに、残る4ヶ所は、町内では宇久須地区の牛越(うしこし)神社と、仁科(中)地区の(海名野)神明神社の2ヶ所、町外では南伊豆町子浦の伊鈴川(五十鈴川/いすずがわ)神社と、伊豆市中伊豆地区の小川神社の2ヶ所<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/ushisanbasou 牛越神社人形三番叟] - 西伊豆町観光協会</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021027.html 海名野神明神社の人形三番叟] - 静岡県</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021025.html 人形三番叟] - 静岡県</ref>。）

=== 祭神 ===
* {{jalink|八幡神|Q261637|廣幡八幡大神}}
* {{jalink|積羽八重事代主命}}

=== 脚注 ===

社名の「佐波（さわ）」は地名の「澤/沢」と同語源<ref name="yuisho"/>。

元々は三島明神と八幡宮が海辺（現・仁科港/鴨ケ池遺跡）に並立していたが、津波の影響で{{jalink|慶長|Q1328185}}14年（{{jalink|1609年|Q6762}}）に現在地に移され、{{jalink|文政|Q1327119}}6年（{{jalink|1823年|Q6988}}）3月に一社に合祀された<ref name="yuisho"/>。

11月2日〜3日の例大祭では、伊豆地区で5ヶ所（町内では3ヶ所）のみが伝承する、県指定無形民俗文化財である「人形{{jalink|三番叟|Q11356796}}」が披露される<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/sawasanbasou 佐波神社三番叟] - 西伊豆町観光協会</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021026.html 仁科の人形三番叟] - 静岡県</ref>。（ちなみに、残る4ヶ所は、町内では宇久須地区の牛越(うしこし)神社と、仁科(中)地区の(海名野)神明神社の2ヶ所、町外では南伊豆町子浦の伊鈴川(五十鈴川/いすずがわ)神社と、伊豆市中伊豆地区の小川神社の2ヶ所<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/ushisanbasou 牛越神社人形三番叟] - 西伊豆町観光協会</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021027.html 海名野神明神社の人形三番叟] - 静岡県</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021025.html 人形三番叟] - 静岡県</ref>。）

=== 祭神 ===

* {{jalink|積羽八重事代主命}}

'''佐波神社'''（さわじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|西伊豆町|Q1203949}}仁科にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|伊豆国の式内社一覧#式内社|Q11380552|伊豆国の式内社}}の1つである<ref name="yuisho">境内由緒書</ref>。

=== 概要 ===
創建不詳だが、{{jalink|式内社}}なので『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』が作られた{{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）以前に遡る<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/神名帳-82644 神名帳とは] - {{jalink|コトバンク|Q11302260}}</ref>。

社名の「佐波（さわ）」は地名の「澤/沢」と同語源<ref name="yuisho"/>。

元々は三島明神と八幡宮が海辺（現・仁科港/鴨ケ池遺跡）に並立していたが、津波の影響で{{jalink|慶長|Q1328185}}14年（{{jalink|1609年|Q6762}}）に現在地に移され、{{jalink|文政|Q1327119}}6年（{{jalink|1823年|Q6988}}）3月に一社に合祀された<ref name="yuisho"/>。

11月2日〜3日の例大祭では、伊豆地区で5ヶ所（町内では3ヶ所）のみが伝承する、県指定無形民俗文化財である「人形{{jalink|三番叟|Q11356796}}」が披露される<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/sawasanbasou 佐波神社三番叟] - 西伊豆町観光協会</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021026.html 仁科の人形三番叟] - 静岡県</ref>。（ちなみに、残る4ヶ所は、町内では宇久須地区の牛越(うしこし)神社と、仁科(中)地区の(海名野)神明神社の2ヶ所、町外では南伊豆町子浦の伊鈴川(五十鈴川/いすずがわ)神社と、伊豆市中伊豆地区の小川神社の2ヶ所<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/ushisanbasou 牛越神社人形三番叟] - 西伊豆町観光協会</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021027.html 海名野神明神社の人形三番叟] - 静岡県</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021025.html 人形三番叟] - 静岡県</ref>。）

{{nihongo|'''Sawa Shrine'''|佐波神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = 佐波神社
|画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|46|38.3|N|138|46|14|E}}}}
|所在地 = {{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|西伊豆町|Q1203949}}仁科1870
|位置 = {{Coord|34|46|38.3|N|138|46|14|E|display=inline,title}}
|祭神   = {{jalink|八幡神|Q261637|廣幡八幡大神}}、{{jalink|積羽八重事代主命}}
|社格   = {{jalink|伊豆国の式内社一覧#式内社|Q11380552|式内社}}、旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}
|創建   = 
|本殿   = 
|別名   = 
|札所等 = 
|例祭   = 11月2日〜3日
|神事   = 
|地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=佐波神社|coordinates=
{{coord2|34|46|38.3|N|138|46|14|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
}}
'''佐波神社'''（さわじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|西伊豆町|Q1203949}}仁科にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|伊豆国の式内社一覧#式内社|Q11380552|伊豆国の式内社}}の1つである<ref name="yuisho">境内由緒書</ref>。

=== 概要 ===
創建不詳だが、{{jalink|式内社}}なので『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』が作られた{{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）以前に遡る<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/神名帳-82644 神名帳とは] - {{jalink|コトバンク|Q11302260}}</ref>。

社名の「佐波（さわ）」は地名の「澤/沢」と同語源<ref name="yuisho"/>。

元々は三島明神と八幡宮が海辺（現・仁科港/鴨ケ池遺跡）に並立していたが、津波の影響で{{jalink|慶長|Q1328185}}14年（{{jalink|1609年|Q6762}}）に現在地に移され、{{jalink|文政|Q1327119}}6年（{{jalink|1823年|Q6988}}）3月に一社に合祀された<ref name="yuisho"/>。

11月2日〜3日の例大祭では、伊豆地区で5ヶ所（町内では3ヶ所）のみが伝承する、県指定無形民俗文化財である「人形{{jalink|三番叟|Q11356796}}」が披露される<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/sawasanbasou 佐波神社三番叟] - 西伊豆町観光協会</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021026.html 仁科の人形三番叟] - 静岡県</ref>。（ちなみに、残る4ヶ所は、町内では宇久須地区の牛越(うしこし)神社と、仁科(中)地区の(海名野)神明神社の2ヶ所、町外では南伊豆町子浦の伊鈴川(五十鈴川/いすずがわ)神社と、伊豆市中伊豆地区の小川神社の2ヶ所<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/ushisanbasou 牛越神社人形三番叟] - 西伊豆町観光協会</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021027.html 海名野神明神社の人形三番叟] - 静岡県</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021025.html 人形三番叟] - 静岡県</ref>。）

=== 祭神 ===
* {{jalink|八幡神|Q261637|廣幡八幡大神}}
* {{jalink|積羽八重事代主命}}

=== 脚注 ===

'''Sawa Shrine''' (さわじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Nishina, {{jalink|Nishiizu|Q1203949}}, {{jalink|Kamo District|Q961511}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}. It is one of the {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province#Shikinaisha|Q11380552|Shikinaisha of Izu Province}}<ref name="yuisho">Precinct history board</ref>.

=== Overview ===
Its foundation is unknown, but because it is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, it dates back to before {{jalink|Enchō (era)|Q1327274|Enchō}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), when the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}'' was compiled<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/神名帳-82644 神名帳とは] - {{jalink|Kotobank|Q11302260}}</ref>.

The shrine name “Sawa” shares its origin with the place name “Sawa/澤”<ref name="yuisho"/>.

Originally, Mishima Myōjin and Hachimangū stood side by side by the sea (present-day Nishina Port/Kamogaike Site), but due to a tsunami they were moved to the current site in {{jalink|Keichō|Q1328185}} 14 ({{jalink|1609|Q6762}}), and were merged into one shrine in March of {{jalink|Bunsei|Q1327119}} 6 ({{jalink|1823|Q6988}})<ref name="yuisho"/>.

At the annual festival on November 2-3, the “Ningyō {{jalink|Sanbasō|Q11356796}},” a prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property preserved in only five places in the Izu area (three in this town), is performed<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/sawasanbasou 佐波神社三番叟] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021026.html 仁科の人形三番叟] - Shizuoka Prefecture</ref>. (For reference, the remaining four places are: in town, Ushikoshi Shrine in Ukusu and (Kainano) Shinmei Shrine in central Nishina; outside town, Isuzugawa Shrine in Koura, Minamiizu, and Ogawa Shrine in the Nakaizu area of Izu City<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/ushisanbasou 牛越神社人形三番叟] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021027.html 海名野神明神社の人形三番叟] - Shizuoka Prefecture</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021025.html 人形三番叟] - Shizuoka Prefecture</ref>.)

=== Enshrined deities ===
* {{jalink|Hachiman deity|Q261637|Hirohata Hachiman Ōkami}}
* {{jalink|Tsumuha-yae Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}

=== Notes ===

At the annual festival on November 2-3, the “Ningyō {{jalink|Sanbasō|Q11356796}},” a prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property preserved in only five places in the Izu area (three in this town), is performed<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/sawasanbasou 佐波神社三番叟] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021026.html 仁科の人形三番叟] - Shizuoka Prefecture</ref>. (For reference, the remaining four places are: in town, Ushikoshi Shrine in Ukusu and (Kainano) Shinmei Shrine in central Nishina; outside town, Isuzugawa Shrine in Koura, Minamiizu, and Ogawa Shrine in the Nakaizu area of Izu City<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/ushisanbasou 牛越神社人形三番叟] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021027.html 海名野神明神社の人形三番叟] - Shizuoka Prefecture</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021025.html 人形三番叟] - Shizuoka Prefecture</ref>.)

'''Sawa Shrine''' (さわじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Nishina, {{jalink|Nishiizu|Q1203949}}, {{jalink|Kamo District|Q961511}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}. It is one of the {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province#Shikinaisha|Q11380552|Shikinaisha of Izu Province}}<ref name="yuisho">Precinct history board</ref>.

=== Overview ===
Its foundation is unknown, but because it is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, it dates back to before {{jalink|Enchō (era)|Q1327274|Enchō}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), when the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}'' was compiled<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/神名帳-82644 神名帳とは] - {{jalink|Kotobank|Q11302260}}</ref>.

The shrine name “Sawa” shares its origin with the place name “Sawa/澤”<ref name="yuisho"/>.

Originally, Mishima Myōjin and Hachimangū stood side by side by the sea (present-day Nishina Port/Kamogaike Site), but due to a tsunami they were moved to the current site in {{jalink|Keichō|Q1328185}} 14 ({{jalink|1609|Q6762}}), and were merged into one shrine in March of {{jalink|Bunsei|Q1327119}} 6 ({{jalink|1823|Q6988}})<ref name="yuisho"/>.

At the annual festival on November 2-3, the “Ningyō {{jalink|Sanbasō|Q11356796}},” a prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property preserved in only five places in the Izu area (three in this town), is performed<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/sawasanbasou 佐波神社三番叟] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021026.html 仁科の人形三番叟] - Shizuoka Prefecture</ref>. (For reference, the remaining four places are: in town, Ushikoshi Shrine in Ukusu and (Kainano) Shinmei Shrine in central Nishina; outside town, Isuzugawa Shrine in Koura, Minamiizu, and Ogawa Shrine in the Nakaizu area of Izu City<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/ushisanbasou 牛越神社人形三番叟] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021027.html 海名野神明神社の人形三番叟] - Shizuoka Prefecture</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021025.html 人形三番叟] - Shizuoka Prefecture</ref>.)

{{coord2|34|46|38.3|N|138|46|14|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}

'''Sawa Shrine''' (さわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Nishina, {{ill|lt=Nishiizu|Q1203949|WD=Q1203949}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}. It is one of the {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province#Shikinaisha|Q11380552|WD=Q11380552|lt=Shikinaisha of Izu Province}}<ref name="yuisho">Precinct history board</ref>.

=== Overview ===
Its foundation is unknown, but because it is a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, it dates back to before {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), when the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'' was compiled<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/神名帳-82644 神名帳とは] - {{ill|lt=Kotobank|Q11302260|WD=Q11302260}}</ref>.

The shrine name “Sawa” shares its origin with the place name “Sawa/澤”<ref name="yuisho"/>.

Originally, Mishima Myōjin and Hachimangū stood side by side by the sea (present-day Nishina Port/Kamogaike Site), but due to a tsunami they were moved to the current site in {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} 14 ({{ill|lt=1609|Q6762|WD=Q6762}}), and were merged into one shrine in March of {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1823|Q6988|WD=Q6988}})<ref name="yuisho"/>.

At the annual festival on November 2-3, the “Ningyō {{ill|lt=Sanbasō|Q11356796|WD=Q11356796}},” a prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property preserved in only five places in the Izu area (three in this town), is performed<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/sawasanbasou 佐波神社三番叟] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021026.html 仁科の人形三番叟] - Shizuoka Prefecture</ref>. (For reference, the remaining four places are: in town, Ushikoshi Shrine in Ukusu and (Kainano) Shinmei Shrine in central Nishina; outside town, Isuzugawa Shrine in Koura, Minamiizu, and Ogawa Shrine in the Nakaizu area of Izu City<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/ushisanbasou 牛越神社人形三番叟] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021027.html 海名野神明神社の人形三番叟] - Shizuoka Prefecture</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021025.html 人形三番叟] - Shizuoka Prefecture</ref>.)

=== Enshrined deities ===
* {{ill|lt=Hachiman deity|Q261637|WD=Q261637|lt=Hirohata Hachiman Ōkami}}
* {{ill||lt=Tsumuha-yae Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}

It is one of the {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province#Shikinaisha|Q11380552|WD=Q11380552|lt=Shikinaisha of Izu Province}}<ref name="yuisho">Precinct history board</ref>.

=== Overview ===
Its foundation is unknown, but because it is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, it dates back to before {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), when the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'' was compiled<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/神名帳-82644 神名帳とは] - {{ill|lt=Kotobank|Q11302260|WD=Q11302260}}</ref>.

The shrine name “Sawa” shares its origin with the place name “Sawa/澤”<ref name="yuisho"/>.

Originally, Mishima Myōjin and Hachimangū stood side by side by the sea (present-day Nishina Port/Kamogaike Site), but due to a tsunami they were moved to the current site in {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} 14 ({{ill|lt=1609|Q6762|WD=Q6762}}), and were merged into one shrine in March of {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1823|Q6988|WD=Q6988}})<ref name="yuisho"/>.

At the annual festival on November 2-3, the “Ningyō {{ill|lt=Sanbasō|Q11356796|WD=Q11356796}},” a prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property preserved in only five places in the Izu area (three in this town), is performed<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/sawasanbasou 佐波神社三番叟] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021026.html 仁科の人形三番叟] - Shizuoka Prefecture</ref>. (For reference, the remaining four places are: in town, Ushikoshi Shrine in Ukusu and (Kainano) Shinmei Shrine in central Nishina; outside town, Isuzugawa Shrine in Koura, Minamiizu, and Ogawa Shrine in the Nakaizu area of Izu City<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/ushisanbasou 牛越神社人形三番叟] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021027.html 海名野神明神社の人形三番叟] - Shizuoka Prefecture</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021025.html 人形三番叟] - Shizuoka Prefecture</ref>.)

'''Sawa Shrine''' (さわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Nishina, {{ill|lt=Nishiizu|Q1203949|WD=Q1203949}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}. It is one of the {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province#Shikinaisha|Q11380552|WD=Q11380552|lt=Shikinaisha of Izu Province}}<ref name="yuisho">Precinct history board</ref>.

=== Overview ===
Its foundation is unknown, but because it is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, it dates back to before {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), when the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'' was compiled<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/神名帳-82644 神名帳とは] - {{ill|lt=Kotobank|Q11302260|WD=Q11302260}}</ref>.

The shrine name “Sawa” shares its origin with the place name “Sawa/澤”<ref name="yuisho"/>.

Originally, Mishima Myōjin and Hachimangū stood side by side by the sea (present-day Nishina Port/Kamogaike Site), but due to a tsunami they were moved to the current site in {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} 14 ({{ill|lt=1609|Q6762|WD=Q6762}}), and were merged into one shrine in March of {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1823|Q6988|WD=Q6988}})<ref name="yuisho"/>.

At the annual festival on November 2-3, the “Ningyō {{ill|lt=Sanbasō|Q11356796|WD=Q11356796}},” a prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property preserved in only five places in the Izu area (three in this town), is performed<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/sawasanbasou 佐波神社三番叟] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021026.html 仁科の人形三番叟] - Shizuoka Prefecture</ref>. (For reference, the remaining four places are: in town, Ushikoshi Shrine in Ukusu and (Kainano) Shinmei Shrine in central Nishina; outside town, Isuzugawa Shrine in Koura, Minamiizu, and Ogawa Shrine in the Nakaizu area of Izu City<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/ushisanbasou 牛越神社人形三番叟] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021027.html 海名野神明神社の人形三番叟] - Shizuoka Prefecture</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021025.html 人形三番叟] - Shizuoka Prefecture</ref>.)

=== Enshrined deities ===
* {{ill|lt=Hachiman deity|Q261637|WD=Q261637|lt=Hirohata Hachiman Ōkami}}
* {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsumuha-yae Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}

Originally, Mishima Myōjin and Hachimangū stood side by side by the sea (present-day Nishina Port/Kamogaike Site), but due to a tsunami they were moved to the current site in {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} 14 ({{ill|lt=1609|Q6762|WD=Q6762}}), and were merged into one shrine in March of {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1823|Q6988|WD=Q6988}})<ref name="yuisho"/>.

At the annual festival on November 2-3, the “Ningyō {{ill|lt=Sanbasō|Q11356796|WD=Q11356796}},” a prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property preserved in only five places in the Izu area (three in this town), is performed<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/sawasanbasou 佐波神社三番叟] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021026.html 仁科の人形三番叟] - Shizuoka Prefecture</ref>. (For reference, the remaining four places are: in town, Ushikoshi Shrine in Ukusu and (Kainano) Shinmei Shrine in central Nishina; outside town, Isuzugawa Shrine in Koura, Minamiizu, and Ogawa Shrine in the Nakaizu area of Izu City<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/ushisanbasou 牛越神社人形三番叟] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021027.html 海名野神明神社の人形三番叟] - Shizuoka Prefecture</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021025.html 人形三番叟] - Shizuoka Prefecture</ref>.)

'''Sawa Shrine''' (さわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Nishina, {{ill|lt=Nishiizu|Q1203949|WD=Q1203949|1=Nishiizu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is one of the {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province#Shikinaisha|Q11380552|WD=Q11380552|lt=Shikinaisha of Izu Province|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province}}<ref name="yuisho">Precinct history board</ref>.

=== Overview ===
Its foundation is unknown, but because it is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, it dates back to before {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), when the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' was compiled<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/神名帳-82644 神名帳とは] - {{ill|lt=Kotobank|Q11302260|WD=Q11302260|1=Kotobank}}</ref>.

The shrine name “Sawa” shares its origin with the place name “Sawa/澤”<ref name="yuisho"/>.

Originally, Mishima Myōjin and Hachimangū stood side by side by the sea (present-day Nishina Port/Kamogaike Site), but due to a tsunami they were moved to the current site in {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 14 ({{ill|lt=1609|Q6762|WD=Q6762|1=1609}}), and were merged into one shrine in March of {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1823|Q6988|WD=Q6988|1=1823}})<ref name="yuisho"/>.

At the annual festival on November 2-3, the “Ningyō {{ill|lt=Sanbasō|Q11356796|WD=Q11356796|1=Sanbasō}},” a prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property preserved in only five places in the Izu area (three in this town), is performed<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/sawasanbasou 佐波神社三番叟] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021026.html 仁科の人形三番叟] - Shizuoka Prefecture</ref>. (For reference, the remaining four places are: in town, Ushikoshi Shrine in Ukusu and (Kainano) Shinmei Shrine in central Nishina; outside town, Isuzugawa Shrine in Koura, Minamiizu, and Ogawa Shrine in the Nakaizu area of Izu City<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/ushisanbasou 牛越神社人形三番叟] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021027.html 海名野神明神社の人形三番叟] - Shizuoka Prefecture</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021025.html 人形三番叟] - Shizuoka Prefecture</ref>.)

(For reference, the remaining four places are: in town, Ushikoshi Shrine in Ukusu and (Kainano) Shinmei Shrine in central Nishina; outside town, Isuzugawa Shrine in Koura, Minamiizu, and Ogawa Shrine in the Nakaizu area of Izu City<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/ushisanbasou 牛越神社人形三番叟] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021027.html 海名野神明神社の人形三番叟] - Shizuoka Prefecture</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021025.html 人形三番叟] - Shizuoka Prefecture</ref>.)

=== Enshrined deities ===

'''Sawa Shrine''' (さわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Nishina, {{ill|lt=Nishiizu|Q1203949|WD=Q1203949|1=Nishiizu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is one of the {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province#Shikinaisha|Q11380552|WD=Q11380552|lt=Shikinaisha of Izu Province|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province}}<ref name="yuisho">Precinct history board</ref>.

=== Overview ===
Its foundation is unknown, but because it is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, it dates back to before {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), when the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' was compiled<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/神名帳-82644 神名帳とは] - {{ill|lt=Kotobank|Q11302260|WD=Q11302260|1=Kotobank}}</ref>.

The shrine name “Sawa” shares its origin with the place name “Sawa/澤”<ref name="yuisho"/>.

Originally, Mishima Myōjin and Hachimangū stood side by side by the sea (present-day Nishina Port/Kamogaike Site), but due to a tsunami they were moved to the current site in {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 14 ({{ill|lt=1609|Q6762|WD=Q6762|1=1609}}), and were merged into one shrine in March of {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1823|Q6988|WD=Q6988|1=1823}})<ref name="yuisho"/>.

At the annual festival on November 2-3, the “Ningyō {{ill|lt=Sanbasō|Q11356796|WD=Q11356796|1=Sanbasō}},” a prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property preserved in only five places in the Izu area (three in this town), is performed<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/sawasanbasou 佐波神社三番叟] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021026.html 仁科の人形三番叟] - Shizuoka Prefecture</ref>. (For reference, the remaining four places are: in town, Ushikoshi Shrine in Ukusu and (Kainano) Shinmei Shrine in central Nishina; outside town, Isuzugawa Shrine in Koura, Minamiizu, and Ogawa Shrine in the Nakaizu area of Izu City<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/ushisanbasou 牛越神社人形三番叟] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021027.html 海名野神明神社の人形三番叟] - Shizuoka Prefecture</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021025.html 人形三番叟] - Shizuoka Prefecture</ref>.)

(For reference, the remaining four places are: in town, Ushikoshi Shrine in Ukusu and (Kainano) Shinmei Shrine in central Nishina; outside town, Isuzugawa Shrine in Koura, Minamiizu, and Ogawa Shrine in the Nakaizu area of Izu City<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/ushisanbasou 牛越神社人形三番叟] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021027.html 海名野神明神社の人形三番叟] - Shizuoka Prefecture</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021025.html 人形三番叟] - Shizuoka Prefecture</ref>.)

'''Sawa Shrine''' (さわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Nishina, {{ill|lt=Nishiizu|Q1203949|WD=Q1203949|1=Nishiizu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is one of the {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province#Shikinaisha|Q11380552|WD=Q11380552|lt=Shikinaisha of Izu Province|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province}}<ref name="yuisho">Precinct history board</ref>.

=== Overview ===
Its foundation is unknown, but because it is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, it dates back to before {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), when the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' was compiled<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/神名帳-82644 神名帳とは] - {{ill|lt=Kotobank|Q11302260|WD=Q11302260|1=Kotobank}}</ref>.

The shrine name “Sawa” shares its origin with the place name “Sawa/澤”<ref name="yuisho"/>.

Originally, Mishima Myōjin and Hachimangū stood side by side by the sea (present-day Nishina Port/Kamogaike Site), but due to a tsunami they were moved to the current site in {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 14 ({{ill|lt=1609|Q6762|WD=Q6762|1=1609}}), and were merged into one shrine in March of {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1823|Q6988|WD=Q6988|1=1823}})<ref name="yuisho"/>.

At the annual festival on November 2-3, the “Ningyō {{ill|lt=Sanbasō|Q11356796|WD=Q11356796|1=Sanbasō}},” a prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property preserved in only five places in the Izu area (three in this town), is performed<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/sawasanbasou 佐波神社三番叟] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021026.html 仁科の人形三番叟] - Shizuoka Prefecture</ref>. (For reference, the remaining four places are: in town, Ushikoshi Shrine in Ukusu and (Kainano) Shinmei Shrine in central Nishina; outside town, Isuzugawa Shrine in Koura, Minamiizu, and Ogawa Shrine in the Nakaizu area of Izu City<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/ushisanbasou 牛越神社人形三番叟] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021027.html 海名野神明神社の人形三番叟] - Shizuoka Prefecture</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021025.html 人形三番叟] - Shizuoka Prefecture</ref>.)

(For reference, the remaining four places are: in town, Ushikoshi Shrine in Ukusu and (Kainano) Shinmei Shrine in central Nishina; outside town, Isuzugawa Shrine in Koura, Minamiizu, and Ogawa Shrine in the Nakaizu area of Izu City<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/ushisanbasou 牛越神社人形三番叟] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021027.html 海名野神明神社の人形三番叟] - Shizuoka Prefecture</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021025.html 人形三番叟] - Shizuoka Prefecture</ref>.)

'''Sawa Shrine''' (さわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Nishina, {{ill|lt=Nishiizu|Q1203949|WD=Q1203949|1=Nishiizu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is one of the {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province#Shikinaisha|Q11380552|WD=Q11380552|lt=Shikinaisha of Izu Province|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province}}<ref name="yuisho">Precinct history board</ref>.

=== Overview ===
Its foundation is unknown, but because it is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, it dates back to before {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), when the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' was compiled<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/神名帳-82644 神名帳とは] - {{ill|lt=Kotobank|Q11302260|WD=Q11302260|1=Kotobank}}</ref>.

The shrine name “Sawa” shares its origin with the place name “Sawa/澤”<ref name="yuisho"/>.

Originally, Mishima Myōjin and Hachimangū stood side by side by the sea (present-day Nishina Port/Kamogaike Site), but due to a tsunami they were moved to the current site in {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 14 ({{ill|lt=1609|Q6762|WD=Q6762|1=1609}}), and were merged into one shrine in March of {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1823|Q6988|WD=Q6988|1=1823}})<ref name="yuisho"/>.

At the annual festival on November 2-3, the “Ningyō {{ill|lt=Sanbasō|Q11356796|WD=Q11356796|1=Sanbasō}},” a prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property preserved in only five places in the Izu area (three in this town), is performed<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/sawasanbasou 佐波神社三番叟] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021026.html 仁科の人形三番叟] - Shizuoka Prefecture</ref>. (For reference, the remaining four places are: in town, Ushikoshi Shrine in Ukusu and (Kainano) Shinmei Shrine in central Nishina; outside town, Isuzugawa Shrine in Koura, Minamiizu, and Ogawa Shrine in the Nakaizu area of Izu City<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/ushisanbasou 牛越神社人形三番叟] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021027.html 海名野神明神社の人形三番叟] - Shizuoka Prefecture</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021025.html 人形三番叟] - Shizuoka Prefecture</ref>.)

(For reference, the remaining four places are: in town, Ushikoshi Shrine in Ukusu and (Kainano) Shinmei Shrine in central Nishina; outside town, Isuzugawa Shrine in Koura, Minamiizu, and Ogawa Shrine in the Nakaizu area of Izu City<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/ushisanbasou 牛越神社人形三番叟] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021027.html 海名野神明神社の人形三番叟] - Shizuoka Prefecture</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021025.html 人形三番叟] - Shizuoka Prefecture</ref>.)

'''Sawa Shrine''' (さわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Nishina, {{ill|lt=Nishiizu|Q1203949|WD=Q1203949|1=Nishiizu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is one of the {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province#Shikinaisha|Q11380552|WD=Q11380552|lt=Shikinaisha of Izu Province|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province}}<ref name="yuisho">Precinct history board</ref>.

=== Overview ===
Its foundation is unknown, but because it is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, it dates back to before {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), when the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' was compiled<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/神名帳-82644 神名帳とは] - {{ill|lt=Kotobank|Q11302260|WD=Q11302260|1=Kotobank}}</ref>.

The shrine name “Sawa” shares its origin with the place name “Sawa/澤”<ref name="yuisho"/>.

Originally, Mishima Myōjin and Hachimangū stood side by side by the sea (present-day Nishina Port/Kamogaike Site), but due to a tsunami they were moved to the current site in {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 14 ({{ill|lt=1609|Q6762|WD=Q6762|1=1609}}), and were merged into one shrine in March of {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1823|Q6988|WD=Q6988|1=1823}})<ref name="yuisho"/>.

At the annual festival on November 2-3, the “Ningyō {{ill|lt=Sanbasō|Q11356796|WD=Q11356796|1=Sanbasō}},” a prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property preserved in only five places in the Izu area (three in this town), is performed<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/sawasanbasou 佐波神社三番叟] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021026.html 仁科の人形三番叟] - Shizuoka Prefecture</ref>. (For reference, the remaining four places are: in town, Ushikoshi Shrine in Ukusu and (Kainano) Shinmei Shrine in central Nishina; outside town, Isuzugawa Shrine in Koura, Minamiizu, and Ogawa Shrine in the Nakaizu area of Izu City<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/ushisanbasou 牛越神社人形三番叟] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021027.html 海名野神明神社の人形三番叟] - Shizuoka Prefecture</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021025.html 人形三番叟] - Shizuoka Prefecture</ref>.)

Originally, Mishima Myōjin and Hachimangū stood side by side by the sea (present-day Nishina Port/Kamogaike Site), but due to a tsunami they were moved to the current site in {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 14 ({{ill|lt=1609|Q6762|WD=Q6762|1=1609}}), and were merged into one shrine in March of {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1823|Q6988|WD=Q6988|1=1823}})<ref name="yuisho"/>.

At the annual festival on November 2-3, the “Ningyō {{ill|lt=Sanbasō|Q11356796|WD=Q11356796|1=Sanbasō}},” a prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property preserved in only five places in the Izu area (three in this town), is performed<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/sawasanbasou 佐波神社三番叟] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021026.html 仁科の人形三番叟] - Shizuoka Prefecture</ref>. (For reference, the remaining four places are: in town, Ushikoshi Shrine in Ukusu and (Kainano) Shinmei Shrine in central Nishina; outside town, Isuzugawa Shrine in Koura, Minamiizu, and Ogawa Shrine in the Nakaizu area of Izu City<ref>[https://www.nishiizu-kankou.com/sightseeing/ushisanbasou 牛越神社人形三番叟] - Nishiizu Tourism Association</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021027.html 海名野神明神社の人形三番叟] - Shizuoka Prefecture</ref><ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/kankosports/bunkageijutsu/bunkazai/1002825/1041003/1041888/1004974/1021025.html 人形三番叟] - Shizuoka Prefecture</ref>.)
